title: The finite mean of a constant signal is the constant

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: On a nonempty finite index set, the finite mean of an identically constant real-valued signal is that constant.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
the real cardinality of s is nonzero

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: finiteDC_const
declarations.statement: theorem finiteDC_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) : finiteDC s (fun _ => c) = (s.card : Real) * c

declarations.name: finiteMean_const
declarations.statement: theorem finiteMean_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : finiteMean s (fun _ => c) = c

tasks.input: Translate the finite mean of a constant signal over a nonempty finite set into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteMean_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : finiteMean s (fun _ => c) = c

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the finite mean, rewriting the constant DC sum, and clearing the nonzero cardinal denominator.
tasks.target: Unfold finiteMean, rewrite finiteDC_const, and use field_simp with hcard.

tasks.input: Find the local lemma that rewrites the DC sum of a constant finite signal.
